name: Mushroom Beef Patties- Part 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine beef, crumbs, milk, Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 teaspoon salt, pepper, and garlic powder in a bowl. Mix well.
2. Shape the mixture into 3 or 4 oval patties.
3. Cook the patties on both sides until no longer pink.
4. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook mushrooms and basil for 2-3 minutes until tender.
5. Increase the heat to high. Add 2 tablespoons butter and stir until most of the liquid evaporates.
6. Melt the remaining butter in the skillet. Stir in flour.
7. Gradually blend in cream. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly.
8. Add 1/2 cup water, hot pepper sauce, and remaining salt to the sauce. Cook and stir for 2 minutes on low heat.
9. If needed, add more water to achieve a medium-thin consistency. Stir in mushrooms. Heat through.
10. Serve the mushroom sauce over beef patties. Top with cheese and onions.
Yield: 3-4 servings.
